summary: Audit Books surface + P2 shell against the OPS design system, 7 dimensions, verified findings

gestalts.verdict: The Books surface itself is largely authentic OPS — earth tones and financial ramps trace cleanly to the semantic/financial token tables, the lone outlined-accent CTA per segment is exactly the spec's primary-button pattern, and numbers are disciplined mono. The knockoff smell comes from the P2 shell: steel-blue accent stripes and glazes permanently parked on the edge-tab rail (navigation chrome, the single most explicitly banned accent placement), brick rendered as text in the top bar, and an improvised third glass recipe plus a fog of off-token greys (0.02–0.14 borders and fills) that softens the spec's whisper-quiet hairline system into generic dark-mode mush. Add text-mute doing real informational work in seventeen places and the shell reads ~85% OPS — the hue family is right, but the discipline that makes the system feel engineered rather than imitated is leaking at exactly the chrome users stare at all day.

Split verdict. The Books surface is a credible OPS build — numbers are mono and formatted, empty values are `—`, the `// TITLE` / 0.16em / 11px micro-label grammar is mostly token-true — but it still leaks at the edges: 12px off-scale labels everywhere, sentences set in JetBrains Mono, and two competing button voices (hand-rolled Cake Mono 12px next to the shared Mohave 16px Button). The P2 shell is where the "cheap replica" reading comes from: 22 type sites below the absolute 11px floor, Cake Mono floating at improvised 10/12/13px, and Mohave moonlighting as the uppercase display face on page titles and breadcrumbs — the three-fonts-three-jobs discipline that defines the system dissolves into a generic "small dark tactical UI" pastiche. Most damning systemically: the spec's non-negotiable slashed-zero feature is enabled nowhere in the product CSS, so every number on every surface renders the wrong zero — the kind of invisible-but-everywhere drift that separates the real instrument from the knockoff.

Split verdict. The Books surface reads authentically OPS on this dimension — `.glass-surface` panels, 2px bars, 4px chips, on-token fills, and not a single box-shadow anywhere in scope, with modals correctly inheriting the shared `.glass-dense` Dialog. The P2 shell is where the replica creeps in: the TopBar invents an entire third glass recipe (rgba(10,10,10,0.25) at blur 12) and stamps it on every control, the sidebar runs the wrong tier, and the hand-built inline-style drawers drift into fractional 2.5px radii, 10px corners on dense glass, and improvised 0.02/0.12/0.14 alphas — exactly the "almost the token, never the token" pattern that makes a trained eye read knockoff. The fix profile is mechanical: collapse the shell back onto the two canonical glass recipes and the seven-value radius scale and the dimension snaps to spec.

On this dimension Books reads as a competent replica, not the real thing: the vocabulary is correct (glass, hairlines, mono micro-labels, left-aligned empty states) but the entire control scale has been miniaturized one tier below spec — a 28px toolbar ladder with 22–24px chips beneath it, 11px row padding, 14px rhythms — values that exist nowhere in DESIGN.md, which is precisely the "cheap knockoff" tell: right shapes, wrong proportions. Critically, the miniaturization is inherited, not invented — table-v2 normalized the 28px tier first and Books faithfully copied it, so this is systemic drift that will keep propagating until the 36px floor is either enforced or formally amended. The P2 shell is the more authentic half (36px menu items and QA rows, 40px top-bar controls, on-grid rail geometry), though its drawers improvise an off-grid inline-style padding dialect and both surfaces center text the system explicitly bans.

The shell's own voice is genuinely OPS — `// TITLE` headers, `[ EOF ]`, `// OPERATOR :: NAME`, em-dash empties, tabular-mono counts like "{n} OF {total}" are confident, terse, and on-spec; nobody faking the system would write the books.json ledger strings. The knockoff tells live at the seams where legacy copy was ported "verbatim" into the new chrome: coach-mark empty states ("Create your first invoice to get started"), Title Case Like This in quick actions and modals, a first-person "we'll be in touch", and a dropped SYS :: prefix the file's own comment promises. The deepest authenticity failure is i18n: the entire P2 notification/quick-actions chrome ships Spanish dictionaries that are admitted EN mirrors with a literal TODO marker, plus en-US-hardcoded currency and untranslated payment terms — a Spanish-locale operator gets a half-English deck, which is exactly what a cheap replica feels like. Fix the ported copy and the es layer and this dimension is authentically OPS; the new-build voice itself is not the problem.

The motion core is genuinely OPS: EASE_SMOOTH is wired as Tailwind's DEFAULT timing function so bare transition-* utilities inherit the house curve, the Books mini-viz (margin meter, aging ramp, diverging bars, sparkline draw-on) lands squarely in the 400-600ms chart band with 50ms/item delays and real reduced-motion gates, and every framer variant ships a Reduced twin. But the highest-traffic entrances betray it — every Books modal and every shell tooltip/undo affordance actually animates on stock ease-out because the keyframe table was never converted, the top bar forgot prefers-reduced-motion entirely, and durations drift by feel (260, 220, 160, 120ms) instead of tracing to the 150/200ms tokens, with the count-up even hand-rolling a second easing curve and annotating it as "adjacent." Disciplined at the center, improvised at the seams — and the seams are what make it read as a near-replica rather than the real instrument.

The Books surface is the closest thing to authentic OPS in this audit — the instrument strip genuinely composes like the kit (// labels with muted slashes, mono tabular heroes, token-bound aging ramps and sparklines, earth-tone status tags, mono-11 table headers over Mohave-14 row names), and it would pass for mission control if its workbar controls weren't shrunken (28px buttons, 12px Cake Mono) and its rows didn't sprout the per-row icon-button toolbars that are the universal generic-admin tell. The knockoff feel concentrates in the P2 shell, which speaks a private dialect of almost-tokens: 9-10px mono dust below the absolute 11px floor, 0.04-0.06 hairlines instead of --line, a blur-12/rgba(10,10,10,0.25) glass recipe the system doesn't define, 2.5px and 8px radii from no table, Cake Mono shrunk to 12-13px, Mohave moonlighting as tracked-uppercase display, and an accent stripe parked permanently on rail chrome that the spec reserves for the single CTA. None of these are wrong-by-much — and that is precisely the cheap-replica signature: everything rendered at ~90% of spec scale and ~60% of spec contrast, so the chrome reads like a careful fan re-creation of the kit rather than the kit itself. Fix the shell's micro-values back to the published tokens and restore the // TITLE mono header and kit row anatomy, and this composition crosses from convincing knockoff to authentic.

confirmedHigh.rule: DESIGN.md §4 Critical rules: "11px minimum. No exceptions." (§15 font size floor: 11px minimum)
confirmedHigh.evidence: <span className="absolute -right-[1px] -top-[1px] flex h-[15px] min-w-[15px] items-center justify-center rounded-[5px] bg-[rgba(255,255,255,0.12)] px-[3px] font-mono text-[9px] leading-none text-text-2 tabular-nums"> — one of 22 sub-11px sites across the P2 shell: sidebar.tsx:186 (9px), 199, 234, 238, 535 (10px); operator-menu.tsx:130, 161 (10px); notifications-drawer.tsx:280, 338, 404, 466, 482 (10px), 443 (9px); notifications-row.tsx:191, 249, 301, 343 (10px — three of these are Cake Mono BUTTON labels at 10px); quick-actions-drawer.tsx:210, 268, 363 (10px), 235, 332 (9px). The Books surface has zero violations of this rule.
confirmedHigh.fix: Raise every site to the 11px floor: Tailwind sites → `text-micro` (11px token); inline-style sites → `fontSize: 11`. For the notifications-row action buttons (191, 301, 343) use the button spec instead: Cake Mono 300 at 14px (`fontSize: 14`). For badges/counts use the 11px badge/metadata tier with tighter padding rather than a smaller font.

confirmedHigh.rule: DESIGN.md §4 Critical rules: "Numbers are always mono. font-feature-settings: \"tnum\" 1, \"zero\" 1. Non-negotiable." — the slashed zero is never enabled on any product surface
confirmedHigh.evidence: <span className="font-mono text-data-lg leading-tight text-text tabular-nums"> — `tabular-nums` sets tnum only; "zero" 1 (slashed zero) is applied nowhere in src/styles/globals.css or tailwind.config.ts (grep confirms the only `"tnum" 1, "zero" 1` rule in product CSS is scoped to `.pmf-scope` in pmf-tokens.css). Every mono number in scope — ledger hero values, invoice/estimate table currency cells, the DeckClock, drawer counts — renders JetBrains Mono's default dotted zero, and any mono number missing the per-callsite `tabular-nums` class silently loses tnum too.
confirmedHigh.fix: Add to globals.css @layer base, mirroring colors_and_type.css:142-145: `.font-mono, code, kbd, samp, pre { font-family: var(--font-mono); font-feature-settings: "tnum" 1, "zero" 1; }` so every `font-mono` site gets tabular-lining + slashed zero without per-callsite classes.

confirmedHigh.rule: DESIGN.md §5: "Two tiers. Glass + hairlines carry all depth." — glass-surface rgba(18,18,20,0.58) blur(28px) saturate(1.3) and glass-dense rgba(18,18,20,0.78) are the ONLY glass recipes. TopBar invents a third tier.
confirmedHigh.evidence: "bg-[rgba(10,10,10,0.25)] backdrop-blur-[12px] [-webkit-backdrop-filter:blur(12px)_saturate(1.1)]" — repeats 4×: top-bar.tsx:68 (SyncIndicator), :194 (hamburger), :265 (undo button), :300 (⌘K search). rgba(10,10,10,0.25) / blur(12px) / saturate(1.1) trace to no token.
confirmedHigh.fix: These are interactive controls, not glass panels: replace with the interactive-surface spec — `bg-surface-input` (rgba(255,255,255,0.04)) rest, `hover:bg-surface-hover`, `border border-line`, drop all backdrop-blur. If a glass chip is truly wanted, it must be `.glass-surface` verbatim.

confirmedHigh.rule: DESIGN.md §9 Buttons: 'border-radius: 5px, min-height: 36px, padding: 9px 16px' — and OPS-Web CLAUDE.md: control sizing traces to the spec (buttons/inputs min-h 36px). There is no 28px control tier anywhere in DESIGN.md, uploads/system.md, or canonical_spec.md.

If a compact toolbar tier is genuinely wanted, it must be ratified into DESIGN.md first — not normalized in code.

confirmedHigh.rule: DESIGN.md §8/§12: "Reduced motion is required. Every animation checks prefers-reduced-motion and falls back to a 150ms opacity transition." Missing fallback = violation.

Reached from books/modals/estimate-form-modal.tsx, invoice-form-modal.tsx, record-payment-modal.tsx.

confirmedHigh.rule: DESIGN.md §8: one easing curve everywhere — the count-up rolls a hand-written quadratic ease-out, a second curve the comment itself admits is only "EASE-SMOOTH-adjacent".
confirmedHigh.evidence: const eased = 1 - (1 - progress) * (1 - progress);  (header comment, line 38: "// ─── Count-up (800ms, EASE-SMOOTH-adjacent quad-out, reduced-motion aware) ───"). Duration (800ms) and the reduced-motion gate are on-spec; the curve is not.
confirmedHigh.fix: Drive the value with framer-motion (already a dependency) so the real curve is used: `animate(from, target, { duration: 0.8, ease: [0.22, 1, 0.36, 1], onUpdate: (v) => setValue(v) })` in place of the rAF loop, or evaluate cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1) exactly instead of the quad-out polynomial.

confirmedHigh.rule: DESIGN.md §3: --ops-accent is "Primary CTA button fill + focus rings. Nothing else." §9: no accent on navigation. The edge-rail tabs are persistent chrome, not CTAs.

Accent appears solely on the one primary CTA per screen and focus rings.

medium.rule: DESIGN.md §5: exactly two glass tiers — rgba(18,18,20,0.58) and rgba(18,18,20,0.78). Every surface color must trace to a token; rgba(10,10,10,0.25) exists nowhere in the token set.
medium.evidence: "bg-[rgba(10,10,10,0.25)] backdrop-blur-[12px] [-webkit-backdrop-filter:blur(12px)_saturate(1.1)]",  — an improvised third glass recipe, repeated ×4 in this file (lines 68, 194, 265, 300) for the sync indicator, hamburger, undo button, and ⌘K search.
medium.fix: Replace with the canonical surface: `bg-[var(--glass)]` (rgba(18,18,20,0.58)) + the .glass-surface blur recipe, or a flat interactive fill `bg-[rgba(255,255,255,0.04)]` if no blur is wanted — no third glass tier.

medium.rule: DESIGN.md §3: earth tones only when color carries the matching semantic meaning — tan = attention/warning, rose = negative/error. Using rose for an attention state makes color lie.
